description_long: This is a large, angular slab of gray stone, fractured into two main pieces with jagged, geometric edges and several smaller rock fragments scattered around its base. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible striations and cracks running through the stone, giving it a weathered appearance. Numerous green vines and patches of leafy vegetation are growing across the rock, clinging to crevices and draping over the edges, suggesting it has been in place for some time. The rock's coloration is predominantly a cool, medium gray, with subtle variations in tone and occasional darker streaks. The overall form is irregular, with one piece leaning against the other, creating a small overhang and shadowed area beneath. The presence of vegetation and debris indicates it is exposed to the elements and not recently disturbed.
description: A large, jagged gray stone slab with rough surfaces and green vines growing over it, split into two main pieces with scattered debris. The rock looks weathered and natural, with visible cracks and leafy vegetation.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main fractured face of the rock, with vines cascading down the sloped surface and smaller rocks at the base.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the angular, geometric top of the rock and the way the two main pieces lean against each other, with vines trailing along the upper edge.
description_view_2: From this angle, the overhang created by the upper slab is visible, as well as more scattered debris and dense vegetation on the lower surface.
description_view_3: The rear view emphasizes the flat, striated surface of the larger slab and the thick cluster of vines and leaves covering the lower portion.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
